creatures.name: Crown Creature
creatures.purpose: Senses the sky and the weather and changes the roof. Mediating the connection between people and the sky, while giving shade and shelter.
creatures.form: A tension structure in a frame. It can fill the whole frame and almost disappear. A fabric is spanned over the tension cable. The roof grows and shrinks in the frame like a breathing lung, while always keeping its geometric ground form which is given by the strong steel cables. The person inside of the fabric plays around with the current form of the structure.
creatures.style: Architecture photography. Parisian park. Lightweight urban structure integrated to the city. Soft fabric surface of hard metal ropes. Functional-surrealist aesthetic.

days.prompt: An orthographic elevation of the structure rendered with brutalist-industrial precision. The fabric surface features a complex, layered fractal pattern, resembling a rigid, metallic-stitched doily. Materials consist of weathered, eroded concrete nodes contrasted against polished, reflective silver tension wires. days.prompt: A low-angle, eye-level street view capturing a rigid, brutalist-industrial canopy. The structure is pierced by two distinct, aggressive jagged steel beams that skewering the core hexagon. The tensile roof is wrapped in a heavy, semi-translucent industrial fabric, patterned with clean, geometric hexagonal fractals. The materiality contrasts weathered, dark industrial steel against the pale, taut textile membranes. High-contrast, sharp geometric shadows are cast onto the Jardin Truillot ground. The scene emphasizes rigid, non-organic angularity, avoiding any fluid curves, focusing on the mechanical collision of structure and membrane.

days.prompt: A low-angle, architectural photograph of the structure in Jardin Truillot. The canopy has evolved into sweeping, wing-like tensile forms that stretch toward the sky. The heavy central mast is removed, replaced by a complex, light-weight steel framework that anchors to the perimeter walls. The fabric membrane is a semi-translucent, high-tech mesh with intricate geometric patterns that cast sharp, layered shadows on the park ground. The overall aesthetic is functional-surrealist, emphasizing the structural contrast between the cold, brushed stainless steel tensile elements and the light, porous canopy. The lighting is flat and diffused, highlighting the crystalline assembly of the joints and the elegant, soaring scale of the structure as it mediates the space between the city and the sky.
days.angle: isolated_perspective from the middle open roof one person.png
days.lens: photograph metal glass building on concrete plinth elegant dynamic form.jpg
days.mutationSummary: Evolving into unfolding wing-like canopy geometry, replacing heavy masts with tension-driven, lightweight structural articulation and translucent solar-patterned membranes.

days.prompt: A low-angle, architectural photograph of a radical tensile structure in Jardin Truillot. The central mast has been fragmented into jagged, metallic shards that act as structural pivots. Spanning between these shards is a crisp, faceted white fabric canopy, stretched taut and sharp, resembling unfolding wings. The base features heavy, industrial steel joints and embedded red-accented panels, reflecting a brutalist-functional aesthetic. Sharp, directional daylight creates high-contrast shadows, emphasizing the geometric precision and the separation of the heavy, rusted-steel anchor points from the delicate, tensioned canopy above. No organic or biological curves; maintain rigid, angular, and industrial articulation.
days.angle: isolated_low angle full view clay day6.png
days.lens: rendering tech color vertical structure with insets.jpg
days.mutationSummary: Shattered geometry replaces the static mast with tensioned, angular wings that unfold to reveal rhythmic, industrial modularity.

The environment is devoid of organic shapes, focusing purely on tectonic collision, hard shadows, and the stark intersection of wire-rigged geometry.

creatures.style: Cinematic urban photography, Parisian street corner. High contrast mood lighting. Surreal, glowing geometric structure radiating light against classical architecture and surroundings. Photorealistic urban aesthetic.

days.prompt: A photorealistic Parisian street corner at twilight. The central structure is composed of rigid, non-organic geometric forms: sharp, angular stone wedges aggressively piercing the historical windows of the surrounding building. Translucent, rectangular glass panels are embedded within a stark metallic framework, radiating a deep, intense glow that saturates the street. Ensure the aesthetic is brutalist and industrial; avoid all soft, biological, or fluid curves. Emphasize the high-contrast lighting, with the structure acting as a glowing neon parasite against the dark, weathered stone of the Boulevard Voltaire. The ground level features sharp, folded metallic planes extending outward to provide shelter.

days.mutationSummary: Sharp stone wedges pierce the Parisian facade; glass panels pulse with internal light, creating jagged, luminous urban tension.

creatures.name: Mist Creature
creatures.purpose: Senses the environment and creates heavy mist. Navigating urban clouds in negotiation with the citizens.
creatures.form: A geometric dynamic form in the middle with a scale that can host one human inside. A curved roof with a visible structure covers the core. creatures.style: Urban photography. Parisian square. Detailed photorealistic urban installation in bright daylight. A complex, metal apparatus generating mist. A sharp, functional-surrealist aesthetic.

creatures.name: Smell Creature
creatures.purpose: Smells the people in the street and analyses the air quality. Identifies specific smells and injects new ones into the environment. Playing with the smell sense of the city.
creatures.form: A parasitic structure placing itself on the sidewalk at the façade. There is a small gap between the structure and the façade where people can pass. Thin panels create a barrier between below and above — between people's individual smell and the city's collective smell. Tentacle-like extensions on the floor reach out into the city to inject new smells through valves.
creatures.style: Architecture photography. Parisian boulevard. Parasitic urban structure integrated into the city. Lightweight structure holding the thin surfaces. Functional-surrealist aesthetic.

days.prompt: A high-fidelity architectural photograph of a parasitic structure attached to a classic Parisian Haussmann facade. The structure is composed of rigid, non-organic, filigree metal components. Large, thin, horizontal aluminum fins extend from a central spine, designed to capture and channel street air. The structure is geometric and deconstructivist, devoid of any fluid or organic shapes. The gears and ground-cracking elements are removed. The surfaces consist of precise, laser-cut metallic grids and porous panels that reflect the ambient light of the boulevard. The central core is tilted at a severe, non-symmetrical angle, replacing previous rhythmic slats with a chaotic, non-repeating array of bent metal blades. High-contrast, dramatic studio lighting emphasizes the sharp reflections on the metal and the deep shadows within the fissure. No organic or curved elements; strict adherence to industrial, functional-surrealist geometry.

days.prompt: A high-contrast architectural rendering in the style of Lebbeus Woods. The central vertical crack in the Parisian Haussmann facade is violently expanded by jagged, oxidized corten steel shards that rip through the masonry. The primary spire is tilted at a sharp, dramatic angle, destabilizing the symmetry. Horizontal metal fins are skewed and rotated, overlapping like serrated blades. The base of the crack is torn open to bleed onto the sidewalk with concrete rubble and exposed steel structural outriggers. Crisp white masonry surrounds the dark, metallic intrusion, with sharp lighting casting long, hard shadows to emphasize the geometric tension and gravity-defying collision.

days.prompt: A symmetrical head-on view of a Haussmann boulevard. A central vertical rupture cuts through the stone facade. Thick, industrial steel plates are violently dislodged and twisted outward at sharp, jagged angles, resembling a mangled, buckled hangar door. The stone masonry around the crack is splintering into jagged shards. Harsh directional sunlight emphasizes the weathering, oxidation, and severe geometric friction of the metallic assembly. No biological, fluid, or organic shapes; only rigid, brutalist tectonic disjunction. The base of the crack transitions into a heap of sharp steel scrap and fractured limestone blocks, emphasizing a seismic-like structural failure.

days.prompt: A direct, eye-level frontal perspective of a Parisian Haussmann facade. A vertical crack in the stone has been forcefully widened by a massive, deconstructivist metallic structure. The central installation consists of sharp, rusted steel plates and shattered angular bracing that violently puncture and displace the limestone blocks. Exposed industrial orange steel beams and jagged metal shards protrude from the fissure, creating a sense of tectonic disjunction. The ground plane is fragmented, with asphalt buckled and uplifted by the structural force. The aesthetic is raw, industrial, and highly kinetic, highlighting the stark material contrast between the historical stone and the sharp, jagged industrial assembly. The lighting is flat and overcast, emphasizing the depth of the fractured void and the harsh geometric precision of the steel intervention.
